title: First Missing Positive
pattern: Array â€” In-place Index Mapping (Cyclic Sort)
coreIdea: Find the smallest missing positive integer in O(n) time and O(1) space by placing each number in its 'correct' index position (value v goes to index v-1) via swapping, then scanning for the first index whose value doesn't match its expected position.
recognitionCues: asks for smallest missing positive integer
O(n) time and O(1) space explicitly required, ruling out sorting or a hashset
approach: For each index i, while arr[i] is in valid range [1, n] and arr[arr[i]-1] != arr[i], swap arr[i] with arr[arr[i]-1] to place it at its correct position. After this placement pass, scan the array; the first index i where arr[i] != i+1 gives the answer i+1. If all match, answer is n+1.
